mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real personal consumption expenditures is a direct measure of aggregate demand strength. When retail sales (RSAFS) increase month-over-month, it signals robust consumer spending, which positively drives corporate earnings and equity valuations over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal produces negative average returns in more than half of out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: A declining unemployment rate (UNRATE) below a threshold of 4.5% indicates a tightening labor market that historically precedes rising wages and consumer confidence, both supportive of equity prices. The signal activates when UNRATE falls below 4.5%, predicting positive S&P 500 forward 20-day returns.
failure_condition: If at any point after the trigger activates, the 20-day forward return is negative in more than 40% of instances, or the average return is not statistically positive at the 5% level over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is low and housing starts (HOUST) are high, the joint signal indicates accommodative monetary policy effectively stimulating the housing sector. This joint condition historically boosts residential investment and related consumer spending, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with housing expansion
failure_condition: The joint signal fails if the average 60-day forward return under the joint condition is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.1) or if the signal decomposes into independent single-series effects that explain the same variance.

mechanism_narrative: Decelerating industrial production (INDPRO) month-over-month growth below 0.2% signals that the supply side of the economy is losing momentum, often a precursor to an earnings slowdown. This negative supply shock, especially in a late-cycle regime, predicts falling equity prices over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the triggered signal produces positive average 20-day returns in any out-of-sample year, or if the false positive rate exceeds 60% (negative prediction but positive actual return).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real purchasing power and prompts expectations of monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuation multiples. Persistent upward drift in the CPI index is a persistent drag on forward 5-day equity returns as market reprices discount rates.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is falsified if the correlation between CPIAUCSL level and forward 5-day S&P 500 returns is non-negative over any 5-year rolling window, or if Rank IC is consistently below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 200,000 signals robust labor demand and income growth. This threshold-triggered signal activates when job creation exceeds that level, historically leading to positive 20-day equity returns as corporate earnings prospects improve.
failure_condition: The signal is rejected if the average 20-day return after trigger events is non-positive, or if more than 50% of triggered instances show negative returns over a 3-year out-of-sample window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous positive month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) confirms broad-based economic expansion without inflationary bottlenecks. This joint signal of supply and labor strength predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as earnings momentum builds.
failure_condition: The interaction hypothesis fails if the joint signal does not outperform a simple sum of individual signals in terms of Rank IC over any 3-year out-of-sample period, or if the average 60-day return is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) exceeds 5.0% in a monetary tightening cycle, it directly raises the risk-free rate and crowds out equity valuations. This regime-conditioned signal triggers in high-rate environments, predicting negative 20-day equity returns as discount rate pressure dominates.
failure_condition: If the average 20-day return after triggering is not negative, or if the proportion of positive returns exceeds 45% in any out-of-sample 2-year roll, the hypothesis is falsified.
